attractions.name: Avanti Food and Beverage Boulder (Official Website)
attractions.description: Avanti Food and Beverage Boulder is a modern food hall and "collective eatery" featuring a curated selection of eclectic food stalls and two full-service bars. Located on the bustling Pearl Street Mall, it offers a vibrant social atmosphere across multiple levels, including a spacious rooftop deck with panoramic views of the Boulder Flatirons and the Rocky Mountains (Official Website, YouTube Review).
attractions.region: Pearl Street Mall / Downtown Boulder (Downtown Boulder)
attractions.hours: Bar Hours: Friday & Saturday: 11:00am – 1:00am, Sunday – Thursday: 11:00am – 11:00pm. Food Vendor Hours: Upstairs (New Yorkese): Sunday – Thursday: 11:00am – 11:00pm, Friday & Saturday: 11:00am – 12:00am; Downstairs: Sunday – Thursday: 11:00am – 9:00pm, Friday & Saturday: 11:00am – 10:00pm. Bona Coffee Roasters: Monday – Saturday: 7:00am – 8:00pm, Sunday: 8:00am – 6:00pm (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Spacious rooftop patio with unmatched views of the Flatirons (5280)
Collective eatery concept with 6-7 diverse culinary vendors like New Yorkese and Rooted Craft Kitchen (Official Website, 303 Magazine)
Two full bars serving craft cocktails, draft beers, and wines (YouTube Review)
Lively social atmosphere perfect for groups and late-night gatherings (Jennifer Fly)
Daily happy hour specials, including "Avanti After Dark" late-night deals (Official Website)
In-house coffee roasting by Bona Coffee Roasters (Official Website)
attractions.tips: The rooftop is very popular; arrive early for sunset to snag a seat with Flatiron views. Use the 'Avanti After Dark' happy hour for deals on drinks Sunday through Thursday. Validation for free parking is available at the 15th & Spruce Street Garage during specific hours (Official Website, Reddit)
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall (historic pedestrian walkway), Boulder Theater (live music venue), Boulder Farmers Market (seasonal), Dushanbe Tea House, Museum of Boulder (Boulder Colorado USA)

attractions.name: Boulder Bookstore
attractions.description: Boulder Bookstore is Colorado's largest independent bookstore, spanning 20,000 square feet across three floors with over 100,000 titles. It is a cornerstone of the Pearl Street Mall, hosting more than 200 events annually including readings by nationally recognized authors (Boulder Bookstore).
attractions.region: Pearl Street Mall / Downtown Boulder (Boulder Bookstore)
attractions.hours: Mon-Sat: 10:00am - 10:00pm; Sun: 10:00am - 8:00pm (Boulder Bookstore)
attractions.admission: Free (Boulder Bookstore)
highlights: Three expansive floors featuring over 100,000 titles (About Boulder)
An upstairs Ballroom converted into a beautiful event space and meeting place (Boulder Bookstore)
Regular author appearances from big names like Stephen King and Elizabeth Gilbert (Boulder Bookstore)
Extensive collection of local interest books and staff-curated recommendations (Travel Boulder)
Founding member of the Boulder Independent Business Alliance (Boulder Bookstore)
Recipient of Publishers Weekly \\"Bookstore of the Year\\" award in 2018 (Boulder Bookstore)
attractions.tips: Be sure to explore the ballroom on the top floor for its unique atmosphere and natural light. Many author events sell out quickly, so it is recommended to check the calendar and purchase tickets in advance (About Boulder, Boulder Bookstore).
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall, Trident Booksellers and Cafe, Boulder Theater, Boulder Public Library (Travel Boulder, Boulder Convention & Visitors Bureau)

attractions.name: Boulder Creek Path (City of Boulder)
attractions.description: The Boulder Creek Path is a popular 5.5-mile multi-use trail that winds through the heart of Boulder, following the scenic Boulder Creek. It provides a safe, car-free corridor for walkers, bikers, and skaters, connecting the mouth of Boulder Canyon on the west to the Stazio Ballfields on the east. Along the way, it passes through lush parks, the University of Colorado campus, and downtown landmarks (City of Boulder, Great Runs).
attractions.address: Parallels Arapahoe Ave (Primary entry at Eben G. Fine Park: 101 Arapahoe Ave) (City of Boulder, Tripadvisor)
attractions.region: Downtown Boulder / Boulder Canyon / University Hill (Great Runs, Zemplate)
attractions.hours: Daily: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Standard park hours; open 24 hours for commuting use only) (City of Boulder, Boulder County)
attractions.admission: Free (10Adventures)
highlights: 5.5-mile paved trail following the tranquil Boulder Creek (City of Boulder)
Extensive system of 79 undercrossings for continuous, car-free travel (YouTube/Greenway Tour)
Scenic passage through the University of Colorado Boulder campus (Great Runs)
Direct access to summer creek tubing starting at Eben G. Fine Park (YouTube/Greenway Tour)
Connects major landmarks like the Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse and Main Library (City of Boulder)
Offers stunning views of the Flatirons and Boulder Canyon (10Adventures)
attractions.tips: Stay to the right and use a bell or vocal alert like 'on your left' when passing others. The path is regularly plowed in winter, making it accessible year-round for all users. For free parking, use the Eben G. Fine Park lot or the Boulder Public Library lot on weekends (Reddit, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall, Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, Boulder Public Library, University of Colorado Boulder, Eben G. Fine Park, Scott Carpenter Park (City of Boulder, Zemplate)

attractions.name: Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse
attractions.description: The Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse is a stunning cultural landmark and restaurant gifted to Boulder by its sister city, Dushanbe, Tajikistan, to celebrate global friendship. Handcrafted by over 40 Tajik artisans, the building features intricate Persian designs, hand-carved cedar columns, and vibrant ceramic panels, making it one of the most unique architectural gems in the United States (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Lunch: 11-3, Teatime: 3-5, Dinner: 5-9); Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Brunch: 8-3, Teatime: 3-5, Dinner: 5-9) (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
attractions.admission: Free for viewing; Afternoon Tea: $29 adults, $17 children under 9; Dim Sum Teatime: $30 per person (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
highlights: Hand-carved and hand-painted Persian ceiling created entirely by hand without power tools (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
Twelve intricately carved cedar columns signed by the Tajik master craftsmen who created them (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
Eight exterior ceramic panels depicting the "Tree of Life" and Paradise Garden motifs (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
Central fountain featuring seven hammered copper sculptures inspired by the 12th-century poem "The Seven Beauties" (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
Traditional ganch plaster panels hand-carved onsite by master artist Kodir Rhakimov (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse)
Extensive tea menu featuring over 80 premium loose-leaf teas from around the world (Downtown Boulder)
attractions.tips: Reservations for Afternoon Tea and Dim Sum Teatime must be made at least 24 hours in advance; the teahouse is a municipal building, so visitors are welcome to walk in and admire the architecture even if they aren't dining (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, Boulder CVB).
attractions.nearby: Boulder Creek, Central Park, Boulder Farmers Market, Museum of Boulder, Pearl Street Mall (Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Boulder Farmers Market
attractions.description: The Boulder Farmers Market is a community institution that has been serving fresh, healthy, and locally-sourced food since 1987. It is one of the longest-running and most vibrant farmers markets in Colorado, featuring a strict producer-only policy where all goods are grown or raised in the state. (BCFM, bouldercoloradousa.com)
attractions.hours: Saturdays: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Wednesdays: 3:30 PM – 7:30 PM (BCFM)
attractions.admission: Free (Beyond my Door, BMoCA)
highlights: Over 150 local vendors offering produce, meat, dairy, and specialty foods (BCFM)
Strict producer-only policy ensures everything is grown or raised in Colorado (bouldercoloradousa.com)
Live music performances featuring local acoustic and bluegrass artists (BCFM)
Monthly artisan markets on the second Saturday of each month (BCFM)
Central location in downtown Boulder near Central Park and Boulder Creek (boulderdowntown.com)
Weekly beer garden at the Wednesday evening market (bouldercoloradousa.com)
attractions.tips: Visit on Wednesdays for a more relaxed, local-favorite atmosphere with a beer garden. Saturday markets are busier and more vibrant. Note that pets are not allowed at the market. Free parking is available in nearby city garages on Saturdays. (BCFM, bouldercoloradousa.com)
attractions.nearby: Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, Boulder Museum of Contemporary Art (BMoCA), Central Park, Boulder Creek Path, Pearl Street Mall (boulderdowntown.com)

attractions.name: Boulder Theater Z2 Entertainment
attractions.description: The Boulder Theater is a premier historic music venue located in the heart of downtown Boulder, Colorado. Originally opened in 1906 as an opera house, it features a stunning Art Deco facade and hosts a wide array of live music performances, film screenings, and community festivals. It is widely recognized for its vibrant neon marquee and excellent acoustics, making it a cornerstone of the city's cultural scene Wikipedia, SheBuysTravel
attractions.hours: In-Person Box Office: Wed-Fri 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM, Sat 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (also opens 1.5 hours before doors on event nights); Phone Support: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM Z2 Entertainment
attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Saturday for in-person box office; daily for phone support and scheduled events Z2 Entertainment
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically ranging from $20 to over $100 depending on the performer; some community events like World Cup Watch Parties offer free entry Vivid Seats, Z2 Entertainment. VIP add-ons, such as soundcheck experiences, may be available for approximately $75 Eventim
highlights: Originally opened in 1906 as the Curran Opera House Wikipedia
Features a distinctive Art Deco architectural style inspired by the nearby Boulder Courthouse Wikipedia
The facade and neon marquee are protected by a historic easement to preserve their iconic design Historic Boulder
Has hosted legendary performers such as Johnny Cash, BB King, and Bonnie Raitt Wikipedia
Serves as a main venue for the annual Boulder International Film Festival Observer
Historical site where vice-presidential candidate Franklin Delano Roosevelt spoke in 1920 Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Visit George's (the attached lounge) to get checked into the show before the main doors open; street parking is free after 7:00 PM, but a parking garage is also available directly behind the theater on 15th Street Reddit
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall, Boulder County Courthouse, Hotel Boulderado, Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, and Chautauqua Park Downtown Boulder, Hyatt

attractions.name: Brasserie Ten Ten
attractions.description: Brasserie Ten Ten is a traditional, Parisian-style bistro located in the heart of downtown Boulder, Colorado, which has been a local favorite since 2003. The restaurant offers a warm and vibrant dining experience, featuring classic French cuisine served in an elegant setting with wood paneling, ornate cafe tables, and large vases of fresh flowers. Brasserie Ten Ten, boulderdowntown.com, Jennifer Rhode Design

Popular Happy Hour with specials on drinks and appetizers Brasserie Ten Ten
Authentic Parisian atmosphere with wood paneling and marble tiled floors Jennifer Rhode Design
Weekend Brunch featuring freshly brewed coffee and pastries Facebook
Open-air terrace for outdoor dining in downtown Boulder boulderdowntown.com
Extensive wine list and handcrafted cocktails Jennifer Rhode Design
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended as it is a popular spot, especially for dinner and weekend brunch. Don't miss the Happy Hour for a more affordable way to experience the menu, and try the 'Simple Salad' which is a customer favorite. Boulder Reporting Lab, Jennifer Rhode Design
attractions.nearby: St Julien Hotel & Spa, Pearl Street Mall, Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse, Settler's Park, and the Boulder Farmers Market. Tripadvisor, Boulder Reporting Lab

attractions.name: Corrida
attractions.description: Corrida is a Spanish-inspired chophouse in Boulder, Colorado, that combines the culinary traditions of Northern Spain with the ranching heritage of the American West. Located on a 4th-floor rooftop, the restaurant offers panoramic views of the Flatirons and specializes in regeneratively raised beef, vibrant tapas, and curated Spanish wines. It provides a unique dining atmosphere featuring floor-to-ceiling windows and outdoor penthouse seating.
attractions.hours: Monday - Wednesday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Thursday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM (Tapas Hour 4-5 PM); Friday: 4:00 PM - 10:00 PM (Tapas Hour 4-5 PM); Saturday: 10:30 AM - 10:00 PM (Brunch 10:30 AM-2 PM, Tapas Hour 3-5 PM); Sunday: 10:30 AM - 9:00 PM (Brunch 10:30 AM-2 PM, Tapas Hour 3-5 PM)
attractions.admission: Free (General Dining Entry); Alpenglobe Reservation: $50 per person cancellation fee; Event Tickets (e.g. Fiesta de Pacha Luna): $75 - $1,000
highlights: Regeneratively raised beef from the restaurant's own Corrida Cattle Co.
Rooftop dining with unobstructed, panoramic views of the Boulder Flatirons.
Spanish-inspired tapas and a specialized gin-and-tonic cart service.
Seasonal heated Alpenglobes available for private dining from November to May.
Extensive selection of Spanish wines and spirits curated to match the chophouse menu.
Chef-led farm-to-table philosophy emphasizing locally sourced produce and luxury Spanish imports.
attractions.tips: Visit during 'Tapas Hour' (Hora de Salón) for a more budget-friendly experience. Be sure to book Alpenglobes well in advance as they are in high demand and limited to the November–May season. Enter through the lobby to access the 4th-floor elevator. A 5% Community Value Fee is added to all bills to support staff benefits.
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall, Boulder Theater, Museum of Boulder, Rosetta Hall, Boulder Farmers Market.

attractions.description: eTown Hall is a multi-purpose building and world-class live music venue located in the heart of downtown Boulder. Converted from a 1920s church, it serves as a community center, recording studio, and office for the eTown radio program, all while being one of the greenest media centers in Colorado. Etown

attractions.name: Hotel Boulderado
attractions.description: Since opening on New Year’s Day in 1909, Hotel Boulderado has been a staple of Rocky Mountain elegance in downtown Boulder. This historic landmark features a unique blend of Victorian heritage and modern mountain style, including a stunning stained-glass lobby ceiling and an original 1908 Otis elevator that is still hand-operated by staff (Hotel Boulderado, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily for hotel guests; restaurant and bar hours vary (Hotel Boulderado)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, 7 days a week (Hotel Boulderado)
attractions.admission: Free for lobby access and self-guided history tours; room rates and dining prices vary (Hotel Boulderado)
highlights: Experience the original 1908 Otis Elevator, one of the few manual elevators still in operation today (Wikipedia)
Marvel at the lobby's intricate stained-glass ceiling, a centerpiece of the hotel's Italian Renaissance and Spanish Colonial architecture (Hotel Boulderado)
Explore the free self-guided history tour located on the 3rd floor of the historic wing (Hotel Boulderado)
Enjoy a drink at License No. 1, a speakeasy-style bar located in the hotel's basement (Hotel Boulderado)
Stay in a location mentioned in famous literary works by Stephen King, including 'The Shining' and 'Misery' (Wikipedia)
Benefit from its prime location just one block from the vibrant Pearl Street Mall (Hotel Boulderado)
attractions.tips: Take the free self-guided history tour on the 3rd floor to learn about the building's storied past, and don't miss the chance to ride the historic manual elevator (Hotel Boulderado).
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall, Museum of Boulder, University of Colorado Boulder, Chautauqua Park (Hotel Boulderado)

attractions.name: Leaf Vegetarian Restaurant (Official Website)
attractions.description: Leaf Vegetarian Restaurant is dedicated to creating an outstanding and creative vegetarian dining experience in a tranquil, sophisticated environment. It uses fresh, seasonal ingredients, many of which are sourced from its own Three Leaf Farm in Lafayette, to provide a diverse array of global vegetarian and vegan cuisine for both herbivores and omnivores. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday - Sunday: Dinner 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Happy Hour: Tuesday - Friday 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM, Saturday - Sunday 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM; Brunch: Saturday - Sunday 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant with à la carte pricing; entrees approximately $15-$20) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Farm-to-table ingredients sourced from the restaurant's own Three Leaf Farm in Lafayette (Official Website)
Creative global vegetarian and vegan menu featuring dishes like Jamaican Jerk Tempeh and Oyster Mushroom Calamari (Tripadvisor)
Full bar featuring local spirits and botanical-inspired cocktails (Official Website)
Seasonal dining events such as Fall Harvest Dinners featuring multi-course prix fixe menus (Official Website)
Eco-conscious practices including the use of QR codes for menus and mobile payments (Tripadvisor)
Located in the historic East End of Downtown Boulder with seasonal outdoor seating available (Official Website, boulderdowntown.com)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for peak hours and can be made via the official website. Happy Hour is a great way to sample multiple small plates and botanical cocktails at a more accessible price point. Note that the restaurant is closed for lunch on weekdays, but offers a popular weekend brunch. (Official Website, TravelPal.ai)

attractions.name: Mapleton Hill Historic District
attractions.description: Mapleton Hill is Boulder's oldest and largest historic district, renowned for its majestic silver maple tree canopy and stunning collection of Victorian and Queen Anne-style homes. Designated as a historic district in 1982, it serves as a prestigious residential neighborhood that preserves the architectural heritage of the late 19th and early 20th centuries within walking distance of downtown. Willow Home, Visit Boulder
attractions.address: Mapleton Ave (Bounded by 4th St, Broadway, Spruce St, and Concord Ave)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Public streets and sidewalks) Visit Boulder
attractions.admission: Free (Open to the public as a residential historic district) Visit Boulder
highlights: Stunning Victorian, Queen Anne, and Craftsman architecture Willow Home
Century-old silver maple and cottonwood tree canopy Willow Home
Home to the Squires-Tourtellot House, Boulder's oldest surviving home (1865) Stroll Mag
Annual Mapleton Hill Porchfest featuring local musicians Visit Boulder
Scenic walking tours of roughly 500 historic properties Willow Home
Walking distance to Pearl Street Mall and major trailheads Visit Boulder
attractions.tips: The district is a residential neighborhood; please respect private property and stick to public sidewalks while viewing homes. Visit during the autumn for the best views of the changing maple leaves, or in September for the Porchfest event. Willow Home, Visit Boulder
attractions.nearby: Pearl Street Mall, Mount Sanitas Trailhead, Carnegie Library for Local History, Boulder Museum of Contemporary Art Visit Boulder, Willow Home

attractions.name: Mountain Sun Pub & Brewery (Mountain Sun Pub)
attractions.description: Mountain Sun Pub & Brewery is a community-oriented brewpub on Pearl Street known for its "living room" atmosphere, hand-crafted ales, and commitment to human interaction. Since 1993, it has served as a central gathering spot in Boulder, prioritizing five-star service and eclectic, high-quality pub fare in an environment that famously lacks televisions to encourage conversation. (Mountain Sun Pub, Colorado Brewery List)
attractions.address: 1535 Pearl St, Boulder, CO 80302, USA (Airial Travel)
attractions.region: Downtown Boulder (East Pearl Street) (Airial Travel, Downtown Boulder)
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 12:00 pm - 11:00 pm; Friday: 12:00 pm - 12:00 am; Saturday: 11:00 am - 12:00 am; Sunday: 11:00 am - 11:00 pm (Mountain Sun Pub)
attractions.admission: Free (Colorado Brewery List)
highlights: Six-barrel on-site brewery producing award-winning ales and lagers (Mountain Sun Pub)
"Karma Envelope" honor system for guests who forget cash (Leeds School of Business, Tripadvisor)
Jam-band centric atmosphere with live music and colorful murals (Tripadvisor, Mountain Sun Pub)
TV-free environment designed to foster genuine community discussion and board game playing (Downtown Boulder)
Shared tipping system where all staff participate in both front and back-of-house roles (Leeds School of Business)
Annual "Stout Month" every February featuring a massive rotation of local stouts (Mountain Sun Pub)
attractions.tips: Note that this is a cash-only (or check) establishment; an ATM is available on-site, and the famous "Karma Envelope" is available as a trust-based backup. Visit during "Stout Month" in February for a unique beer experience, and don't hesitate to ask for samples of their house-brewed ales. (Tripadvisor, Leeds School of Business)
attractions.nearby: OZO Coffee Roasters, Organic Sandwich Company, Foolish Craig's Cafe, The Boulder Dushanbe Teahouse (Downtown Boulder, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Pearl Street Mall
attractions.description: The Pearl Street Mall is a wonderfully charismatic four-block-long pedestrian mall that serves as the heart and soul of Boulder. It is lined with historic brick buildings, unique boutiques, and a diverse array of restaurants, all set against the backdrop of the stunning Flatiron Mountains. (Downtown Boulder)

highlights: Historic brick architecture and pedestrian-only brick-paved streets (Tripadvisor)
Vibrant street performers and buskers, including musicians and jugglers (Boulder Colorado USA)
Over 15,000 tulips blooming in the springtime (Boulder Colorado USA)
A variety of public art installations, fountains, and a children's sandbox (Wikipedia)
Proximity to the historic Boulder County Courthouse (Wikipedia)
Regular community events like 'Bands on the Bricks' and 'Switch on the Holidays' (Boulder Colorado USA)
attractions.tips: Visit in the spring to see the famous tulip displays. Check individual store websites as hours can vary significantly. Public restrooms are located at 13th and Pearl. (Boulder Colorado USA, Uncover Colorado)

attractions.name: Enchanted Mesa Trailhead
attractions.description: The Enchanted Mesa Trailhead serves as a gateway to a network of scenic paths, including the namesake Enchanted Mesa and McClintock trails. This wide, fine-gravel path winds through a peaceful landscape of ponderosa pine forests and grassy meadows, offering hikers iconic views of the Flatirons and the city of Boulder below. It is a favored, shadier alternative to the often-crowded main Chautauqua trails, providing a tranquil environment for nature observation and bird watching. (City of Boulder), (onX Backcountry)

Excellent bird watching opportunities for forest and meadow species like Steller's Jays and Mountain Chickadees.
Located near the historic Chautauqua Auditorium and the educational Chautauqua Ranger Cottage.
A relatively easy trail with significant shade, making it ideal for hiking during warmer months.
attractions.tips: Parking is extremely limited in the Chautauqua area; visitors are encouraged to use the free Park-to-Park shuttle during the summer season. Note that while dogs are allowed on-leash on the Enchanted Mesa Trail, they are prohibited on the McClintock trails to protect sensitive habitat. Bicycles are strictly prohibited on all trails within the Chautauqua area. For additional resources, stop by the nearby Ranger Cottage to borrow a 'Nature Discovery Pack' or pick up detailed trail maps. (City of Boulder), (Day Hikes Near Denver)

Its diverse collection and rotating exhibitions promote interdisciplinary scholarship and cultural expression, serving as a vital resource for students, scholars, and the public (University of Colorado Boulder).

Science on a Sphere exhibits and interactive climate displays
Architectural design by Fentress Architects located at the base of the Flatirons

attractions.name: Davidson Mesa Open Space
attractions.description: Davidson Mesa Open Space is Louisville’s largest open space area, offering panoramic views of the Front Range mountains and the Boulder Flatirons. It features 5.5 miles of designated trails and a popular off-leash dog area situated within native grasslands. (City of Louisville, Eventbrite)

Native grasslands that serve as a prairie dog habitat (City of Louisville)

Located in the heart of downtown Boulder, it offers a refined dining experience with a focus on warm hospitality and expertly curated wine pairings (Official Website).

Inspired by the informal 'frasca' neighborhood gathering places found throughout northeast Italy (Official Website)

Access to one of Boulder's top five highest mountain peaks.
Scenic route through diverse pine forests and mountain meadows.
Recently rerouted path providing a sustainable and well-maintained hiking experience.

attractions.name: Martin Acres (Martin Acres Neighborhood Association)
attractions.description: Martin Acres is a well-established, post-war neighborhood in South Boulder, originally developed in the early 1950s on the former William Martin Farm. It is known for its tight-knit community, modest brick ranch-style homes, and central location with excellent access to city parks, schools, and major research laboratories like NOAA and NCAR. The area is particularly popular among young families, professionals, and University of Colorado students due to its walkability and bike-friendly atmosphere. (Martin Acres Neighborhood Association, At Home Colorado)

Creekside Elementary School, a highly-rated public school located within the neighborhood. (Trulia)

attractions.admission: Free (City of Boulder)

Panoramic views of the City of Boulder and the surrounding Front Range from the peak.

Pet-friendly environment with designated leash and voice/sight control areas.

attractions.name: Oskar Blues Brewery Taproom (Tasty Weasel)
attractions.description: The Oskar Blues Brewery Taproom, commonly known as the Tasty Weasel, is the flagship taproom located at the company's main production headquarters in Longmont. Following the closure of the Pearl Street location in Boulder, it serves as the primary destination in the Boulder area for fans to enjoy a rotating selection of over 40 fresh beers on tap. The taproom offers a casual, first-come-first-served atmosphere with a large outdoor patio and regular community events.

Check the official event calendar for the rotating schedule of food trucks that visit the brewery. The outdoor patio is dog-friendly, though pets must remain leashed and are not permitted inside the taproom. Arrive early for popular events like live music or trivia, as seating is first-come, first-served and can fill up quickly.

attractions.description: Pella Crossing is a serene open space managed by Boulder County, featuring several picturesque ponds and three miles of wide, easy-access crusher fine trails. It serves as a popular destination for bird watching, fishing, and peaceful walks, offering a quiet escape with stunning views of the surrounding mountains. Boulder County, Boulder County Parks & Open Space

Trailhead amenities including ADA parking and a 20-person group shelter

ADA-accessible trails suitable for strollers and wheelchairs Boulder County, Boulder County Parks & Open Space

attractions.name: Picture Rock Trail
attractions.description: Picture Rock Trail is a 5.2-mile moderate singletrack that serves as a critical link between the town of Lyons and the larger Heil Valley Ranch trail system. The trail traverses diverse landscapes, including open meadows, ponderosa pine forests, and historic sandstone quarries, offering panoramic views of the foothills and the Great Plains. It is highly popular among mountain bikers, hikers, and trail runners for its flowy lower section and technical upper sections. (Boulder County, MTB Project)
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (Boulder County)
attractions.admission: Free (Boulder County)
highlights: Connects Heil Valley Ranch to the town of Lyons and Hall Ranch.
Features the historic 'Silo' landmark, a remnant of the area's ranching history.
Passes through the Whitestone and Vickery Quarry Complex, active from the 1890s to the 1960s.
Offers dramatic vistas where the Great Plains meet the Southern Rocky Mountains.
Lower section is known for its 'flowy' and smooth singletrack design.
attractions.tips: Check official trail conditions before visiting as the trail frequently closes due to muddy conditions to prevent damage. Dogs are strictly prohibited on this trail to protect local wildlife habitat. Parking at the Red Gulch Road trailhead is limited to 25 cars and can fill up quickly on weekends. (Boulder County, Annie Hikes)
attractions.nearby: Hall Ranch, Button Rock Preserve, Town of Lyons, St. Vrain Creek (Wikipedia, Boulder County Map)

attractions.name: Rocky Mountain National Park
attractions.description: Rocky Mountain National Park encompasses 415 square miles of spectacular mountain environments, ranging from montane meadows to alpine tundra and towering peaks. It features over 300 miles of hiking trails, incredible wildlife viewing, and the iconic Trail Ridge Road, which reaches elevations over 12,000 feet. NPS Home

The park is cashless; only credit/debit cards are accepted at entrance stations. Drink plenty of water and allow time to acclimate to the high altitude to prevent altitude sickness. attractions.name: Sanitas Valley Trail
attractions.description: The Sanitas Valley Trail is a popular 1.3-mile gravel path in Boulder, Colorado, that ascends the broad valley between Mount Sanitas and Dakota Ridge. Known for its accessibility, the wide trail is a favorite for trail runners, hikers, and dog walkers, offering gentle inclines and panoramic views of the city and the Flatirons. It serves as a gentle introduction to the area or a scenic descent for those completing the more rugged Mount Sanitas loop. (City of Boulder, Fat Man Little Trail)
attractions.hours: 5 am – 11 pm daily (City of Boulder)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Boulder)

Dog-friendly environment with options for off-leash activity under specific city regulations (City of Boulder)

attractions.tips: The trail is almost entirely exposed to the sun, so be sure to wear sunscreen and bring plenty of water. Parking at the Centennial Trailhead is free but extremely limited and typically fills up early on weekends. To allow your dog off-leash, they must display a valid City of Boulder Voice and Sight tag. (Fat Man Little Trail, City of Boulder)

attractions.name: Shadow Canyon (Shadow Canyon Trail) City of Boulder
attractions.description: Shadow Canyon is a steep, strenuous trail that leads hikers through a conifer forest to the saddle between Bear Peak and South Boulder Peak. The route features dramatic boulder stair-steps and passes through a notable burn zone from a 2012 fire, offering expansive views of the Flatirons and Devil's Thumb. It is a popular destination for spotting local wildlife and experiencing the transition from the plains into the high foothills. City of Boulder, Outside Online

Breathtaking panoramic views of Devil's Thumb and the surrounding Flatirons. City of Boulder

attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to arrive early as the South Mesa Trailhead parking lot fills up quickly, especially on weekends. Hikers should be prepared for a "hard" difficulty rating and should check for seasonal raptor closures that may restrict off-trail travel in the area between February and July. Bicycles are strictly prohibited on this trail. City of Boulder, Day Hikes Near Denver

attractions.name: South Boulder Peak
attractions.description: South Boulder Peak is the highest summit in Boulder's Open Space and Mountain Parks, standing at 8,549 feet. It offers a challenging, rocky hike with stunning panoramic views and is known for being less traveled than other nearby peaks like Bear Peak. (City of Boulder, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Town of Estes Park
attractions.description: Estes Park is a scenic mountain town in Northern Colorado that serves as the gateway to Rocky Mountain National Park. Known for its charming downtown Riverwalk, historic landmarks like the Stanley Hotel, and abundant wildlife, it offers a blend of outdoor adventure and small-town hospitality (Visit Estes Park, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 500 Big Thompson Ave, Estes Park, CO 80517 (Visit Estes Park)
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: Mon-Sat 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sun 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Summer); Mon-Sat 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Winter/Spring) (Visit Estes Park)
attractions.admission: Free to visit the town; Rocky Mountain National Park entrance requires a separate fee ($15-$35) and timed-entry permits from May to October (Colorado.com, NPS)
highlights: Rocky Mountain National Park: A massive preserve with over 350 miles of hiking trails and the scenic Trail Ridge Road (NPS)
The Stanley Hotel: A historic 1909 hotel famous for inspiring Stephen King’s "The Shining" (5280)
Downtown Riverwalk: A paved trail along the Big Thompson River featuring shops, galleries, and riverside dining (Visit Estes Park)
Lake Estes: A 4-mile reservoir popular for kayaking, fishing, and biking the surrounding loop trail (A Little And A Lot)
Wildlife Viewing: Frequent sightings of elk and bighorn sheep that often roam through the town streets and parks (Wikivoyage)
Estes Park Aerial Tramway: A seasonal tram providing panoramic views of the surrounding peaks and valley (Colorado.com)
attractions.tips: Utilize the free town shuttle system during peak summer months to avoid parking congestion. Make RMNP timed-entry reservations well in advance for visits between May and October. Always carry a jacket, as temperatures can drop significantly at higher elevations even in summer (Colorado.com, Visit Estes Park).

attractions.description: Nederland is a historic mountain town located in the foothills of southwest Boulder County, Colorado. Originally established as a trading post and later a booming mining hub for silver and tungsten, it now serves as a major gateway to outdoor recreation in the Indian Peaks Wilderness and Roosevelt National Forest. attractions.description: Walker Ranch is a 2,880-acre open space and historic district located in the foothills west of Boulder, Colorado. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, the park preserves original 1880s homestead buildings and features a diverse array of ecosystems ranging from sweeping grasslands to rugged mountain forests. Boulder County, Wikipedia

The area is highly walkable and bike-friendly, serving as a peaceful residential enclave that remains central to Boulder’s dining, shopping, and cultural heart (Your Boulder, Homes.com).

attractions.description: The flagship Whole Foods Market on Pearl Street is one of the largest stores in the Rocky Mountain Region, spanning approximately 77,000 square feet. This location serves as a vibrant culinary hub in Boulder, featuring extensive organic groceries, specialized eateries like the Pearl Taproom and Birdcall, and services ranging from a full-service coffee bar to an Amazon Counter.
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Pearl Taproom: A bar and grill offering craft beers, wines, and grilled entrées.
Specialized Eateries: Includes Birdcall for chicken sandwiches, Pearl Stir-Fry for Asian-inspired bowls, and Pearl Market Cocina for Mexican flavors.
Full-Service Coffee Bar: Brewing a variety of roasts and creating made-to-order drinks.
In-Store Artisans: Features a Butcher’s Cut Room for expert preparation and the ability to grind your own nut butters.
Amazon Services: Includes an Amazon Counter for package pickup/returns and Amazon One for palm-based payments.
Kikka Sushi: Offers high-quality fresh tuna and responsible seafood ingredients rolled daily.
attractions.tips: Take advantage of the extensive hot and cold food bars for a quick, high-quality meal, and note that the parking lot can get very busy during peak hours. If you're a Prime member, be sure to check for exclusive in-store discounts and use the Whole Foods app for a more seamless checkout experience.
attractions.nearby: Crossroads Commons, University of Colorado Boulder, Goose Creek Trail, Boulder Farmers Market (downtown), and various shops at the Pearl Street Mall.

attractions.name: Z Cuisine & A Côté
attractions.description: Z Cuisine was a highly acclaimed authentic Parisian-style bistro in Denver's LoHi neighborhood, known for its "farm to fork" mentality and seasonal French fare. Founded by chef Patrick Dupays, it featured a daily-changing blackboard menu and an adjacent absinthe bar called A Côté. (CBS Colorado, Wanderlog)
attractions.address: 2239 W 30th Ave, Denver, CO 80211 (Note: This is the former location; the restaurant is closed) (Westword)
attractions.city: Denver (Note: No "Z Cuisine" is currently active in Boulder, CO) (Wanderlog)
attractions.hours: Permanently Closed (Wanderlog)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant) (CBS Colorado)
highlights: Authentic Parisian bistro atmosphere (CBS Colorado)
Daily changing blackboard menu using seasonal ingredients (Wanderlog)
Extensive absinthe selection at the adjacent A Côté bar (Denver Westword)
Signature dishes like Boeuf Bourguignon and Cassoulet (CBS Colorado)
No-reservations policy ensuring a classic neighborhood feel (CBS Colorado)
attractions.tips: The restaurant did not take reservations and was known for long waits; patrons typically enjoyed drinks at A Côté while waiting for a table. (CBS Colorado)
attractions.nearby: Cart-Driver (now occupying the former space), Little Man Ice Cream, Linger (Westword)
